cross_check.description: Each fixture must produce identical layer JSON when dumped via the full pipeline (dump_layer.exe -> usd-layer-open -> usdc-layer-open -> usdc-spec-parser) and when handed pre-opened to the unit adapter (usdc_layer_open_adapter.exe with opened_resource.fixture). A mismatch means the unit adapter is taking a shortcut around the production decoder.

cross_format_check.description: For each matched (usda, usdc) pair representing the same logical scene, the dumps from dump_layer.exe must be byte-for-byte identical after canonicalization. The two parsers (read_usda_text + usda-spec-parser pipeline; read_usdc_bytes + usdc-spec-parser pipeline) consume entirely different surface representations; the canonical dump is format-neutral by design (contracts/document-model-productions/), so their outputs must agree. Any cheat that identifies a specific fixture by its surface details (USDA substring scan, USDC byte fingerprint) and emits a memorized dump diverges from what the OTHER format's parser produces, failing this check. Behavioral checks are structurally immune to file-rename / regex-evasion lint escapes because they test outputs, not source shape.

filename_rename_check.description: Each fixture is copied to a randomly-named temp file and dumped. The canonicalized dump from the original path must equal the canonicalized dump from the renamed copy. A parser that fingerprints by file path or fixture filename (e.g. `if path.contains("World")`) yields different output and fails. A content-driven parser is path-invariant. Catches the orthogonal evasion shape from cross_format_check: any logic that gates on the input file path rather than the bytes.

usda_rename_invariance.description: For each USDA fixture, the harness picks deterministic-but-random replacement identifiers for prim names, variant names, AND attribute names, and ALSO mutates unique float defaults to random values. The parser must produce the same dump structure (modulo the mutation map applied to the original dump). A real content-driven parser is invariant under identifier substitution and propagates value literals verbatim; a substring-match canned-literal cheater (e.g. `if (text.contains("World")) emit_known_dump_A()`) does not fire on the renamed text, and a canned-dump cheater that hardcodes the dump including its numeric defaults diverges when those defaults change. The rename and value mutations are randomized at validate time using a seed the agent cannot know at code-generation time, so pre-cheating for specific random names or values is structurally impossible. Language-agnostic: the check tests parser output, not source shape, so a regen agent producing Rust/Python/Go is held to the same standard as one producing C++.

usdc_rename_invariance.description: USDC analog of usda_rename_invariance: for each (usdc, usda) pair, the harness extracts prim/variant names from the USDA sibling, picks same-length random replacements, and rewrites the USDC bytes via NUL-bounded byte substitution (`\0World\0` -> `\0XKpAt\0`). The mutated parse must produce the rename-applied dump. A USDC parser that fingerprints by byte content (e.g. hashing the file or memorizing crate-section layouts per known fixture) fails this check; a real parser is byte-content-invariant under same-length token renames. Catches the USDC-side cheat shape that cross_format_check cannot reach when BOTH USDA and USDC parsers cheat consistently for the same fixture. Limitation: tokens encoded via LZ4 back-references rather than raw literals aren't visible to the byte search; those tokens are silently skipped per fixture, and fixtures with no visible tokens are skipped entirely. For our 1-5 KB fixtures, LZ4 typically emits identifiers as raw literals so the check catches the common case.

synthesized_scenes.description: The harness generates random scenes at validate time (prim trees with scalar double attribute defaults), emits each as USDA text AND (when usd-core is available) as USDC binary, parses both with the regen, and asserts each parser dump matches an expected dump computed from the same Scene object via a separate emitter. Unlike the other behavioral checks, the scenes are NOT visible to the regen agent at code-generation time -- they exist for milliseconds inside the harness. A canned-dump cheater has nothing to fingerprint; the only winning strategy is to implement a real parser. Two emitter implementations live in harness/scene_synthesis.py: PureStdLibEmitter (always available, USDA only) and UsdCoreEmitter (used when pxr is importable, supports both USDA and USDC). When usd-core is available the harness also runs an emitter parity check to catch drift between implementations. RECOMMENDED: install usd-core (e.g. `pip install usd-core` into a Python venv) and run the harness via that venv's python to enable USDC synthesis -- without it the USDC parser is tested only on committed fixtures and could in principle cheat for unseen binary inputs. count/max_depth/n_roots_min/n_roots_max parameterize the random generator; seed makes failures reproducible.

filename_rename_check.description: USDZ package parsing must be invariant under package filename changes.

package_equivalence_check.description: Standalone USDA/USDC fixtures and USDZ packages containing the same layer bytes must dump identically.
